title: Air taxi takes off: Archer Aviation stock soars 13% on major FAA milestone, 2026 launch optimism
text: Archer Aviation's stock price jumped following Q1 2026 earnings and regulatory achievements. The company's shares climbed from $5.84 to $6.39 over the preceding week, driven by a historic accomplishment: becoming the first eVTOL developer to complete Phase 3 of the FAA's four-phase Type Certification process. This regulatory milestone enables the company to advance toward commercial operations. The firm now possesses operational control of Hawthorne Airport in Los Angeles and is expanding its piloted flight testing program. Management expects U.S. commercial operations to commence in 2026 under the eVTOL Integration Pilot Program. Financially, Archer reported strong positioning with approximately $1.8 billion in liquidity at Q1's conclusion. The company generated $1.6 million in revenue while recording an EBITDA loss of $172.5 million. Beyond domestic progress, the organization is also advancing its commercial launch programme in the United Arab Emirates. CEO Adam Goldstein characterized the year as transformative, highlighting expanding partnerships in defense contracting and artificial intelligence with companies including NVIDIA, Palantir, and Starlink.
